title: Methodological Framework
description: A structured programming approach. This is the level where session allocation decisions are made — how many of what type of session per week, and in what proportion.

source_philosophy.description: ID of the philosophy this framework derives from. Must match a philosophy.id.

goals_served.description: Goal profile IDs or modality IDs this framework is designed to support.

sessions_per_week.description: How many sessions of each type this framework prescribes per week. Keys are modality IDs or session type labels.

intensity_distribution.description: What fraction of sessions fall into each intensity zone. Fractions should sum to 1.0.

progression_model.description: ID of the progression model that governs advancement in this framework.

applicable_when.description: Conditions under which the system should select this framework. All specified conditions must be met.

goal_priority_min.description: Minimum priority weight required in the goal profile for any modality listed.

incompatible_with.description: Other framework IDs that cannot run concurrently with this one, with a reason.

interference_level.description: low = monitor; manageable = apply mitigation rule; high = do not combine

mitigation.description: What to do if both are needed anyway. e.g. 'Use SS on strength days, CFE rules on conditioning days'

deload_protocol.description: How a deload week looks within this framework.

frequency_weeks.description: Deload every N weeks. e.g. 4 = deload on week 4 of every block.

volume_reduction_pct.description: Reduce volume to this fraction of peak week. e.g. 0.6 = 60% of normal.

intensity_change.description: What happens to intensity during deload.

cadence_options.description: Day-of-week patterns per training frequency. Keys are integers (days/week); values are lists of day-number lists (1=Mon, 7=Sun). Multiple options rotate week-to-week.
